title: dryopteris sparsa subsp. nitidula
intro: Dryopteris sparsa subsp. nitidula, also known as a rare fern species, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the Dryopteris genus. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant are believed to include relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as soothing skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
